title: Geometry, Scale, Similarity, and Spatial Planning · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Create a scale plan for an accessible room layout with paths, clearances, paint, and flooring. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an accessible scale floor plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify angles and two- and three-dimensional shapes by defining properties rather than appearance.
Choose and justify perimeter or area models for borders, surfaces, and composite regions.
Choose volume or surface-area models for capacity, covering, and packaging tasks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.instruction: A rectangular working area measures 7 m by 5 m. Calculate its area. Use the result to decide Similarity Pythagorean for an accessible scale floor plan.
steps.why: The Similarity Pythagorean result for an accessible scale floor plan is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 7 m × 5 m. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Before calculating Similarity Pythagorean, predict the sign and approximate magnitude for an accessible scale floor plan. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then reconcile it with that prediction and A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use similarity and the Pythagorean theorem only when their geometric conditions hold.

hints: Multiply perpendicular lengths and square the length unit: 7 m × 5 m.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes m² or m^2 or square meters.
